items.title: Add isolated Python/JAX prototype rewrite under new subdirectory
items.body:
### Motivation

- Provide an incremental, clean-room path to modernize ELLE process code by demonstrating a process-level rewrite in Python with JAX acceleration.
- Keep the original C/C++ code untouched by placing the prototype in a separate `python_jax_model/` subdirectory.

### Description

- Add a small multiphase grain-growth simulator implemented in `python_jax_model/elle_jax_model/simulation.py` with a JAX-first backend and a NumPy fallback for environments without JAX.
- Add a CLI runner `python_jax_model/run_simulation.py` that saves `.npy` snapshots and a `README` and `requirements.txt` documenting usage and dependencies.
- Add lightweight unit tests `python_jax_model/tests/test_simulation.py` that check initialization normalization and output shapes, plus a `.gitignore` for runtime artifacts.

### Testing

- Ran `python -m compileall python_jax_model` which completed successfully. 
- Ran `PYTHONPATH=python_jax_model pytest -q python_jax_model/tests`; an initial collection run failed due to missing `jax`/`numpy`, after adding a NumPy fallback and installing `numpy` the test suite passed with `2 passed`.

------

items.title: Fix vulnerable pip dependencies: Pillow, python-jose, python-multipart
items.body:
Three backend dependencies had known CVEs. Updated to patched versions in `backend/requirements.txt`.

## Changes

- **Pillow** `10.3.0` → `12.1.1` — out-of-bounds write when loading PSD images (affects `>= 10.3.0, < 12.1.1`)
- **python-jose** `3.3.0` → `3.4.0` — algorithm confusion vulnerability with OpenSSH ECDSA keys
- **python-multipart** `0.0.9` → `0.0.22` — arbitrary file write via non-default config + DoS via malformed `multipart/form-data` boundary

items.title: refactor: consolidate duplicate Python helpers into pkg/tools/python/
items.body:
## What Changed

Five Python venv/pip orchestration helpers duplicated between the hook executor (python_executor.go) and the framework service ( ramework_service_python.go) are now shared through pkg/tools/python/. Both consumers already depended on this package, so no new dependency edges were introduced. This eliminates ~80-100 lines of duplication and ensures future changes only need to happen in one place.

## How It Works

Extracted shared logic into exported functions/methods on python.Cli: VenvNameForDir() for naming, EnsureVirtualEnv() for stat→create, InstallDependencies() for pip/pyproject dispatch, ResolveCommand() for py/python/python3 resolution, and VenvPythonPath()/VenvActivateCmd() for platform-aware venv paths. The executor's stricter implementation (with rrors.Is and directory type checks) was used as the canonical version. The pythonTools interface was simplified accordingly.

## Issue References

Fixes #7570
Relates to #7435

items.title: Feature/python sdk
items.body:
## Description
This pull request introduces comprehensive support for Python within Matimo, bringing the Python SDK to full feature parity with the TypeScript SDK. It updates documentation, CI/CD workflows, and developer tooling to support both TypeScript and Python, and adds new workflows for testing and publishing the Python SDK and providers. The most important changes are grouped below:

**Python SDK introduction and documentation:**
- Added references to the Python SDK in `README.md`, including installation instructions, minimal usage example, and updated feature lists to reflect full Python support. Also added links to the Python SDK documentation. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-b335630551682c19a781afebcf4d07bf978fb1f8ac04c6bf87428ed5106870f5R13-R17) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-b335630551682c19a781afebcf4d07bf978fb1f8ac04c6bf87428ed5106870f5R39-R48) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-b335630551682c19a781afebcf4d07bf978fb1f8ac04c6bf87428ed5106870f5L57-R82) [[4]](diffhunk://#diff-b335630551682c19a781afebcf4d07bf978fb1f8ac04c6bf87428ed5106870f5R103) [[5]](diffhunk://#diff-b335630551682c19a781afebcf4d07bf978fb1f8ac04c6bf87428ed5106870f5R124) [[6]](diffhunk://#diff-b335630551682c19a781afebcf4d07bf978fb1f8ac04c6bf87428ed5106870f5L155-R182) [[7]](diffhunk://#diff-b335630551682c19a781afebcf4d07bf978fb1f8ac04c6bf87428ed5106870f5R197-R214) [[8]](diffhunk://#diff-b335630551682c19a781afebcf4d07bf978fb1f8ac04c6bf87428ed5106870f5L254) [[9]](diffhunk://#diff-b335630551682c19a781afebcf4d07bf978fb1f8ac04c6bf87428ed5106870f5R346)

**CI/CD and workflow enhancements:**
- Updated `.github/workflows/ci.yml` to split TypeScript and Python testing into separate jobs, added Python linting, type checking, and coverage reporting, and upgraded all GitHub Actions to their latest major versions. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-b803fcb7f17ed9235f1e5cb1fcd2f5d3b2838429d4368ae4c57ce4436577f03fL10-R24) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-b803fcb7f17ed9235f1e5cb1fcd2f5d3b2838429d4368ae4c57ce4436577f03fL34-R34) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-b803fcb7f17ed9235f1e5cb1fcd2f5d3b2838429d4368ae4c57ce4436577f03fL43-R101)
- Added `.github/workflows/test-python.yml` for dedicated Python SDK testing on pushes and pull requests, including linting, type checking, and coverage upload.
- Added `.github/workflows/publish-python.yml` to automate publishing of the core Python SDK and provider packages to PyPI, with test gates and OIDC trusted publishing.
- Updated `.github/workflows/npm-release.yml` to ensure all steps run in the `typescript` directory and upgraded actions for consistency. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-aad3b21c2ed02fc778a85762a45f71b12bd5b6ee7732ca75c6cb46d5d73aa485L21-R27) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-aad3b21c2ed02fc778a85762a45f71b12bd5b6ee7732ca75c6cb46d5d73aa485L38-R59)
- Improved `.github/workflows/tool-validation.yml` to validate tool YAML files in both TypeScript and Python provider directories, and run all steps in the appropriate working directory. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-599a11e3cd7eb62323ba7191ee96c466f6ab932b6cd90433770f96f17439b28eL6-R7) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-599a11e3cd7eb62323ba7191ee96c466f6ab932b6cd90433770f96f17439b28eR41-R49)
- Added `.github/workflows/update-changelog.yml` to automatically prepend release notes to the changelog on new releases.

**Developer tooling improvements:**
- Enhanced `.husky/pre-commit` and `.husky/pre-push` hooks to lint, format, and test both TypeScript and Python codebases, conditionally running Python checks if `uv` is available. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-d2bc4bbf14eadc292d84e0ef5f7a93115c23557f533809fc80b896934291529dL3-R17) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-43bbc75a027a43baaddc57670d8ab49b4aa2981053c3cabba17567723d3a9fb6L3-R20)

These changes ensure that the project now fully supports Python alongside TypeScript, with robust CI/CD, clear documentation, and improved developer experience.

items.title: Add HSI adapter and .mat dataset support for adapting RGB flow to hyperspectral inverse problems
items.body:
### Motivation
- Adapt a pretrained RGB flow model to solve hyperspectral (HSI) inverse problems by mapping RGB flow outputs to HSI feature space and then to HSI channels. 
- Keep the heavy pretrained RGB flow frozen and only train a small adapter so transfer is lightweight and stable. 
- Let inverse-problem training update the adapter online using a data-fidelity loss and support input HSI data stored as `.mat` files in `./data/`.

### Description
- Added a lightweight adapter module `HyperspectralFlowAdapter` (`pnpflow/hsi_adapter.py`) that maps flow RGB outputs -> eigen-image features (configurable `r` via `hsi_eigen_channels`) -> HSI channels using `1x1` convolutions. 
- Integrated the adapter into the PNP flow pipeline (`pnpflow/methods/pnp_flow.py`) by freezing the pretrained flow backbone, forwarding only the RGB subset (`fm_input_channels`) into the flow, and projecting the flow output to HSI via the adapter; adapter parameters are updated online with a fidelity loss (`0.5 * ||H(x_pred)-y||^2 / sigma^2`) using an adapter optimizer. 
- Added `.mat` dataset support via `HSIMatDataset` and a `hsi_mat` branch in `DataLoaders` (`pnpflow/dataloaders.py`) that locates 3D cubes in `.mat` files (configurable `hsi_mat_key`), converts to `C×H×W` and normalizes each sample to `[-1, 1]`. 
- Wired `main.py` to pass `args` into `DataLoaders` so HSI-specific config fields are available, and added `config/dataset_config/hsi_mat.yaml` with tunable fields: `hsi_eigen_channels`, `fm_input_channels`, `adapter_lr`, `hsi_data_dir`, and `hsi_mat_key`.

### Testing
- Performed a Python syntax check with `python -m py_compile main.py pnpflow/dataloaders.py pnpflow/methods/pnp_flow.py pnpflow/hsi_adapter.py` which completed successfully. 
- No additional automated unit tests were present or run in this patch.

------

This pull request contains small formatting improvements to both the main protocol logic and its associated tests. The changes focus on improving code readability by reformatting conditional statements and function calls.

- Code formatting improvements:

  * Reformatted function calls in the `test_handle_p2p_response_collects_and_updates_confidence` test in `test_snowball.py` to use multi-line formatting for better clarity.

This PR contains the following updates: | Package | Change | [Age](https://docs.renovatebot.com/merge-confidence/) | [Confidence](https://docs.renovatebot.com/merge-confidence/) | |---|---|---|---| | [pytest](https://redirect.github.com/pytest-dev/pytest) ([changelog](https://docs.pytest.org/en/stable/changelog.html)) | `9.0.2` → `9.0.3` | ![age](https://developer.mend.io/api/mc/badges/age/pypi/pytest/9.0.3?slim=true) | ![confidence](https://developer.mend.io/api/mc/badges/confidence/pypi/pytest/9.0.2/9.0.3?slim=true) | ### GitHub Vulnerability Alerts #### [CVE-2025-71176](https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-71176) pytest through 9.0.2 on UNIX relies on directories with the `/tmp/pytest-of-{user}` name pattern, which allows local users to cause a denial of service or possibly gain privileges. --- ### Release Notes
pytest-dev/pytest (pytest) ### [`v9.0.3`](https://redirect.github.com/pytest-dev/pytest/releases/tag/9.0.3) [Compare Source](https://redirect.github.com/pytest-dev/pytest/compare/9.0.2...9.0.3) ### pytest 9.0.3 (2026-04-07) #### Bug fixes - [#​12444](https://redirect.github.com/pytest-dev/pytest/issues/12444): Fixed `pytest.approx` which now correctly takes into account `~collections.abc.Mapping` keys order to compare them. - [#​13634](https://redirect.github.com/pytest-dev/pytest/issues/13634): Blocking a `conftest.py` file using the `-p no:` option is now explicitly disallowed. Previously this resulted in an internal assertion failure during plugin loading. Pytest now raises a clear `UsageError` explaining that conftest files are not plugins and cannot be disabled via `-p`. - [#​13734](https://redirect.github.com/pytest-dev/pytest/issues/13734): Fixed crash when a test raises an exceptiongroup with `__tracebackhide__ = True`. - [#​14195](https://redirect.github.com/pytest-dev/pytest/issues/14195): Fixed an issue where non-string messages passed to unittest.TestCase.subTest() were not printed. - [#​14343](https://redirect.github.com/pytest-dev/pytest/issues/14343): Fixed use of insecure temporary directory (CVE-2025-71176). #### Improved documentation - [#​13388](https://redirect.github.com/pytest-dev/pytest/issues/13388): Clarified documentation for `-p` vs `PYTEST_PLUGINS` plugin loading and fixed an incorrect `-p` example. - [#​13731](https://redirect.github.com/pytest-dev/pytest/issues/13731): Clarified that capture fixtures (e.g. `capsys` and `capfd`) take precedence over the `-s` / `--capture=no` command-line options in `Accessing captured output from a test function `. - [#​14088](https://redirect.github.com/pytest-dev/pytest/issues/14088): Clarified that the default `pytest_collection` hook sets `session.items` before it calls `pytest_collection_finish`, not after. - [#​14255](https://redirect.github.com/pytest-dev/pytest/issues/14255): TOML integer log levels must be quoted: Updating reference documentation. #### Contributor-facing changes - [#​12689](https://redirect.github.com/pytest-dev/pytest/issues/12689): The test reports are now published to Codecov from GitHub Actions. The test statistics is visible [on the web interface](https://app.codecov.io/gh/pytest-dev/pytest/tests). \-- by `aleguy02`

Updates `pyrefly` from 0.58.0 to 0.59.1
Release notes

Sourced from pyrefly's releases.

Pyrefly v0.59.1

Fixed a performance regression in 0.59.0.

Full Changelog: https://github.com/facebook/pyrefly/compare/0.59.0...0.59.1

Pyrefly v0.59.0

Status : Beta
Release date: March 30, 2026

Pyrefly v0.59.0 bundles 153 commits from 20 contributors.

✨ New & Improved

Area What’s new
Type Checking - You can now use while...else statements with returns in the else clause without triggering a false positive missing-explicit-return error. - Pyrefly now correctly handles type inference for nested empty dictionaries when constructing TypedDict instances, avoiding implicit-any errors. - Error messages now highlight related code with inline labels; for example, an unsupported * operation will show the types of both operands directly in the source snippet
Language Server - LSP hover information for classes now displays constructor signature and docstring. - Support additional LSP functionality for notebooks, including find-references and rename.
Performance - Faster typechecking in large pythonc codebases, up to 2x faster on recent benchmarks on real world projects - Reduced CPU usage through smarter caching of module resolution results - Improved performance of the LSP server by reducing redundant workspace diagnostic publishes.

🐛 bug fixes

We closed 16 bug issues this release 👏

  • #2026: Fixed an issue where recursive bounded generics were incorrectly reported as object, ensuring accurate type checking.
  • #2812: Resolved a false positive invalid-type-var error when persisting the get method of a fully-annotated dict.
  • #2804: Fixed an implicit-any false positive that occurred with TypedDict items, improving code readability.
  • #2868: Pyrefly now correctly recognizes while...else statements with returns in the else clause as exhaustive.
  • #2814: Enhanced hover information for datetime.datetime imports to display constructor signatures and docstrings.
  • #2896: Fixed a bad-argument-type error that occurred when using double-underscore arguments.
  • #2893: Pyrefly now correctly handles dict Literal key types as subtypes of str key types.
  • #2865: Resolved an issue where tuple subclasses with overridden __getitem__ were not recognized.
  • #2871: Fixed a false positive error when using isinstance with type | X.
  • And more! #2444, #1270, #2900, #2862, #2853

Thank-you to all our contributors who found these bugs and reported them! Did you know this is one of the most helpful contributions you can make to an open-source project? If you find any bugs in Pyrefly we want to know about them! Please open a bug report issue here

📦 Upgrade

pip install --upgrade pyrefly==0.59.0

Updates `pytest` from 9.0.2 to 9.0.3
Release notes

Sourced from pytest's releases.

9.0.3

pytest 9.0.3 (2026-04-07)

Bug fixes

  • #12444: Fixed pytest.approx which now correctly takes into account ~collections.abc.Mapping keys order to compare them.

  • #13634: Blocking a conftest.py file using the -p no: option is now explicitly disallowed.

    Previously this resulted in an internal assertion failure during plugin loading.

    Pytest now raises a clear UsageError explaining that conftest files are not plugins and cannot be disabled via -p.

  • #13734: Fixed crash when a test raises an exceptiongroup with __tracebackhide__ = True.

  • #14195: Fixed an issue where non-string messages passed to unittest.TestCase.subTest() were not printed.

  • #14343: Fixed use of insecure temporary directory (CVE-2025-71176).

Improved documentation

  • #13388: Clarified documentation for -p vs PYTEST_PLUGINS plugin loading and fixed an incorrect -p example.
  • #13731: Clarified that capture fixtures (e.g. capsys and capfd) take precedence over the -s / --capture=no command-line options in Accessing captured output from a test function <accessing-captured-output>.
  • #14088: Clarified that the default pytest_collection hook sets session.items before it calls pytest_collection_finish, not after.
  • #14255: TOML integer log levels must be quoted: Updating reference documentation.

Contributor-facing changes

  • #12689: The test reports are now published to Codecov from GitHub Actions. The test statistics is visible on the web interface.

    -- by aleguy02
